title: DiskLogStrategy.getLogFile does not handle folder.mkdirs() failure
body: In DiskLogStrategy.java's WriteHandler.getLogFile(), there's a TODO comment '//TODO: What if folder is not created, what happens then?' right after folder.mkdirs() -- if mkdirs() returns false (e.g. no storage permission or read-only external storage), the method proceeds to build a FileWriter against a nonexistent directory, which then fails inside handleMessage's try/catch and the log line is silently dropped with no indication why.

title: getLogFile scans the whole logs directory linearly on every single log write
body: WriteHandler.getLogFile() in DiskLogStrategy.java increments newFileCount and calls new File(folder, ...).exists() in a while loop starting from 0 every time a log message is written. Once a device has accumulated hundreds of logs_N.csv rotation files, every single Logger call pays an O(n) directory stat cost just to find the current file, which visibly slows down verbose logging sessions.

title: PrettyFormatStrategy CHUNK_SIZE comment says ~4076 bytes but constant is 4000 with no citation
body: PrettyFormatStrategy.java documents CHUNK_SIZE = 4000 with a comment claiming 'Android's max limit for a log entry is ~4076 bytes', but doesn't explain why 4000 (not 4076) was chosen as the safety margin, and multi-byte UTF-8 characters near the boundary could still be split mid-codepoint since chunking appears to operate on char index rather than byte length.

title: AndroidLogAdapter does not expose a way to disable pretty borders per log call
body: AndroidLogAdapter.java wraps a FormatStrategy for every isLoggable/log call uniformly; there's no per-call override to fall back to a plain one-line format for a single Logger.d(...) invocation without constructing an entirely separate Logger instance with a different FormatStrategy.

title: CsvFormatStrategy quoting doesn't escape embedded commas or quotes in log messages
body: CsvFormatStrategy.java writes log rows for DiskLogAdapter without apparent CSV-escaping of the message field; a log message containing a comma or double-quote (very common when logging JSON payloads) would corrupt the resulting CSV's column alignment when opened in a spreadsheet tool.
